title: Silence MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warnings on ESM planning scripts
description: Node.js emits MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON on every run of scripts/planning/*.js because these ESM files sit under a package.json that lacks "type":"module". Fix: add marker files scripts/planning/package.json + scripts/planning/lib/package.json, each {"type":"module"}. SoNash carries the lib one already. Non-blocking; cosmetic noise only.

title: SoNash file registry — full migration-opportunity map
description: Build a systematic registry mapping every file in SoNash with: source path, upstream callers, downstream deps, workflows touched, sanitization hits (per PORT_ANALYSIS extended regex), migration necessity verdict (port / sanitize-port / redesign / skip / defer), and rationale. Input may come from an outside source (external repo, CLI tool, or standalone generator). Goal: replace ad-hoc per-port MI-1 pre-analysis with a one-shot comprehensive scan that feeds sync-mechanism research (MI-3) AND guides Layer 2/3/4 gating decisions. Natural pair with sync-mechanism brainstorm and the propagation-patterns subsystem (D37).

title: CLAUDE.md §3 Architecture fill-in
description: Populate CLAUDE.md §3 Architecture section (currently TBD placeholder). Deferred per D4 during Foundation discovery — §1 Stack and §2 Security landed in Layer 0+ item 0j, but §3 was held until architecture emerges from sync-mechanism research. Trigger: post-sync-mechanism-research, when the propagation/sync/memory architecture has actually taken shape in the repo.

title: Memory bidirectional sync
description: Implement bidirectional sync for .claude/canonical-memory/ ↔ ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/. Currently D27 landed one-way canonical-first with manual cp as onboarding step. Research caveat CH-C-010 notes session-local memory fix does not solve portability. Trigger: sync-mechanism research addresses memory as part of core scope — natural pairing since both are propagate-changes-across-locations problems.

title: Port _shared/SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN.md + _shared/SKILL_STANDARDS.md from SoNash
description: 7 references from skill-audit SKILL.md and REFERENCE.md point into .claude/skills/_shared/ which does not exist in JASON-OS (dead links). Raised 2026-04-17 during Layer 0+ item 0f port. Options: (A) port both files from SoNash, (B) rewrite refs to drop _shared/ paths (content duplication). Recommendation A. Trigger: Layer 3 engagement (SKILL_INDEX.md pairs naturally with _shared/) OR first skill port needing SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN or SKILL_STANDARDS for MI-5 sub-step.

title: Port or rewrite npm run skills:validate refs in skill-audit
description: 5 advisory references in skill-audit/SKILL.md point to a SoNash npm script not in JASON-OS. Currently non-blocking (advisory if-you-have-this-command prompts). Users running skill-audit will see missing-npm-script errors if they follow the refs. Options: (A) port skills:validate to JASON-OS as Layer 3 validation tooling, (B) rewrite refs with manual equivalents, (C) skill-audit refresh from later SoNash branch with stack-agnostic equivalent. Raised 2026-04-17 during 0f.

title: Gemini CLI integration in /pr-review
description: Add Gemini as a third AI reviewer voice (alongside Qodo + SonarCloud) in /pr-review. Deferred per D23: minimum set for MI-4 was Qodo+SonarCloud only. CodeRabbit excluded (durable preference — SoNash experience with overlap/noise). Gemini CLI v0.38.1 already installed locally; OAuth pending (user-action). Trigger: after Qodo+SonarCloud stabilized and user wants another reviewer voice.

title: pr-retro skill port
description: Port pr-retro from SoNash (deferred in .planning/jason-os/BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md during original Foundation bootstrap). Trigger: pr-ecosystem-audit skill exists (coupling needed for full SoNash version) OR pr-retro simplified to a standalone retro skill that does not require pr-ecosystem-audit.

title: synthesize + recall skill ports from SoNash
description: Neither skill currently exists in JASON-OS .claude/skills/ (which holds 9 skills + add-debt stub). SoNash has active uncommitted upgrade work on both (second-locale work) as of Step 2 check. Not in Foundation firm or gated scope. Raised 2026-04-17 during Step 2. Trigger: (a) SoNash upstream commits land for synthesize + recall — user will signal; AND (b) JASON-OS scope expands to include either — probably coupled to sync-mechanism research per MI-3 since synthesize specifically touches the propagation pipeline.

title: runAnalyze mandatory mode flip
description: user-prompt-handler currently advisory per D6 + D25 (stderr hints only). Flip to mandatory mode via env var JASON_ANALYZE_MODE=mandatory (1-line change thanks to D25 parameterized design — dispatch table extracted to .claude/analyze-directives.json). Trigger: ≥5 agents have PROACTIVELY clauses stable + workflow pattern stabilized (≥10 sessions using advisory mode).

title: Husky pre-commit enrichment (14 deferred checks)
description: Composite entry for 14 deferred pre-commit checks from D36-revised: ESLint, tests, lint-staged, pattern-compliance, propagation-staged, audit-s0s1, skill-validation, cognitive-complexity, cross-doc-deps, doc-headers, doc-index auto-update, agent compliance (non-blocking), debt-schema, JSONL-MD sync. Layer 0+ item 0h laid down the husky scaffold so each addition is single-line. Per-item triggers (e.g., ESLint when stack declared JS/TS; audit-s0s1 when audit system lands). Break into individual todos as each trigger fires.

title: Husky pre-push enrichment (11 deferred checks)
description: Composite entry for 11 deferred pre-push checks from D36-revised: circular-deps, pattern-compliance-push, code-reviewer gate, propagation full, hook-tests, security-check, tsc, cyclomatic-CC, cognitive-CC, npm-audit, event-triggers. Husky scaffold (0h) + escalation-gate stanza already present. Per-item triggers (e.g., tsc when TypeScript stack; code-reviewer gate when track-agent-invocation ported in Layer 2). Break into individual todos as each trigger fires.

title: Pattern cognition & propagation subsystem — full port (D37)
description: Full port of SoNash subsystem: 8 scripts + JSON registry + test suite + 7 npm scripts. Seeded by Layer 0+ item 0i (scripts/config/propagation-patterns.seed.json with 4 anti-patterns from CLAUDE.md §5). Pairs naturally with sync-mechanism research per MI-3 — both are propagate-changes-across-locations. Trigger: After Foundation firm layers complete AND sync-mechanism research begins.

title: session-end-commit.js SESSION_CONTEXT.md format compatibility
description:
Ported session-end-commit.js from SoNash 41526 expects the literal string `**Uncommitted Work**: Yes` (inline bold-colon format) but JASON-OS SESSION_CONTEXT.md uses the SoNash 5-field `## Header
Value` format per D12. Script logs `Could not find Uncommitted Work field` and returns false. Fix: either (a) update the script to parse the `## Uncommitted Work
<value>` format, or (b) rewrite a JASON-OS-native session-end-commit.js. Discovered during first real /session-end invocation 2026-04-17. Non-blocking — main session currently handles session-end git orchestration directly.

title: Validate commit-tracker + pre-compaction-save hooks fire under live Claude Code invocation
description: Session 2 fixed a stdin/argv mismatch in both Layer 1 hooks (commit 4db5afb): they were ported from SoNash reading process.argv[2], but Claude Code passes hook context as JSON via stdin. All 4 manual stdin scenarios pass and artifacts (commit-log.jsonl, handoff.json) write correctly. Could not validate live in Session 2 because Claude Code caches the hook config from settings.json at session start, and this session started on the pre-Layer-1 startup-41526 branch. After next Claude Code restart on bootstrap-41726, validate by: (1) make any commit, verify .claude/state/commit-log.jsonl appends a new entry; (2) for PreCompact, harder to trigger naturally — verify .claude/state/handoff.json appears after natural compaction or via /checkpoint. If either fails, the next debugging vector is the if: clause in settings.json (PostToolUse Bash) — may not be valid Claude Code syntax; falling back to no if: + script-internal regex filter is the simplest workaround.
